title: Patch-clamp recordings of SNpc dopamine neurons from midbrain slices
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"51dfg","text":"Protocol for patch-clamp recordings of SNpc dopamine neurons from midbrain slices","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Place a midbrain slice containing the SNpc in the recording chamber of a setup equipped with an uprightmicroscope (Nikon Eclipse FN1) and infrared video-camera (CoolSNAP Photometrics).

Perfuse the recording chamber at 2.5–3.0 mL/min with aCSF (33.0 ± 0.5°C) containing (in mM): NaCl 126, NaHCO 3 24, glucose 10, KCl 2.5, CaCl 2 2.4, NaH 2 PO 4 1.2 and MgCl 2 1.2 (95% O 2 –5% CO 2 , pH 7.3).

Identify SNpc dopamine neurons based on their morphology (fusiform, tightly packed, and medium to large-sized cell bodies (diameter ≥ 20 μm)), localization (in the medial SNpc, close to the medial terminalnucleus of the accessory optic tract, MT), and their typical electrophysiological features, as slow spontaneous firing (1–8 Hz) in cell-attached configuration, large action potential (> 2ms) with a prominent afterhyperpolarization, or the presence of a prominent hyperpolarization-activated inward current (I h ) in response to hyperpolarizing voltage steps.

Perform patch-clamp recordingsby using with a Multiclamp 700B amplifier (Molecular Devices, USA) using Clampex software (Molecular Devices, USA) and the A/D converter Digidata 1440A (Molecular Devices, USA) connected to a computer

Perform path-clamprecording with glass borosilicate pipettes (4–7 MΩ) filled with a solution containing (in mM): 125 K-gluconate, 10 KCl, 10 HEPES, 2 MgCl 2 , 4 ATP-Mg 2 , 0.3 GTP-Na 3 , 0.75 EGTA, 0.1 CaCl 2 , 10 Phosphocreatine-Na 2 (pH 7.3 with 2 M KOH)
Record the spontaneous firing activity by cell-attached configuration for at least 2-3 minutes

Record in whole-cell configuration the passive membrane properties (membrane resistance (R m ) and capacitance (C m )) with the membrane test protocol (5 mV hyperpolarizing step from -60 mV, at 33.3 Hz by averaging 100 consecutive measurements to calculate final values)
Elicit and record I h by applying a voltage-clamp protocol of four hyperpolarizing voltage steps(-60/-120 mV, 20 mV increment, for 1 sec, VH= -60 mV) to SNpc DA neurons
Elicit and record the afterhyperpolarization-associated current (I AHP ) by applying adepolarizing voltage step (from V H = -60 mV to 0 mV, 100 ms)
creator: Ada Ledonne
